narrative.summary:
📍 L'Avenue on Parliament
583 Parliament St, Toronto, ON M4X 1P9, Canada

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're on Parliament just south of Wellesley, where the Victorian rowhouses still have their original storefronts. Inside, the decor leans French bistro—expect dark wood, good lighting that makes morning feel optimistic, and tables close enough that you'll overhear your neighbor's weekend plans. The room has that polished neighborhood spot energy where regulars read the paper alone and groups linger over second coffees.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: The brunch menu runs classic French-leaning: eggs Benedict variations with properly poached eggs, though the hollandaise can be timid. Their Lyon version comes with sweet brioche and thick-cut ham that doesn't hold back on portion size. Expect to spend around $18-22 for mains, and skip the orange juice—it tastes like it came from a carton despite the 'fresh-squeezed' claim.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐ "I came here for brunch today ordered got the Lyon eggs Benedict. The fruit was fresh and the food overall was delicious. I liked the sweetness of the bread that they had the poached eggs on and they gave a hearty portion of the ham on top. The hollandaise was a bit of a lighter, weaker flavor but I still enjoyed it. Their freshly squeezed orange juice tasted like it was storebought though, save your money. Service overall was great and I loved the decor in the restaurant."

⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Lovely dinner at l'Avenue last night. Had the crab cakes, which were nicely presented but a bit too dense, not crispy, and pricey for the quality. Escargot needed a bit more garlic in our view as did the Caesar salad which was creamy but needed a bit more of a kick.
The star of the show was their homemade lobster spaghetti which came with a full lobster tail and was made with a lobster bisque sauce...very yummy.
Service was excellent and very attentive!
We look forward to brunch there in a few weeks."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Our first dinner there was indulgent and worth it. Food is prepared exquisitely. The ambiance is sophisticated but cozy. Had 4 courses for a birthday celebration including fresh oysters, French onion soup, bone in rib eye for 2 (enough for 3 and took the rest home), with dessert soufflé for 2 (was divine; not too sweet and light) in addition to 2 cocktails and a bottle of red wine (medium prices available). Service was attentive, and polite with attention to detail. Was $436 for 2 of us including everything but could pare it down if not wanting to spend as much. Would definitely return and look forward to brunch on their nice patio in the warmer weather."
